_comment: Seccomp profile for Ubuntu LXC container on Android GSI.
Strategy: default ALLOW with targeted DENY for dangerous operations.
This preserves Ubuntu package compatibility while blocking kernel-level attacks.

syscalls._comment: DENY: Kernel module operations — prevent loading malicious modules
DENY: kexec — prevent replacing running kernel
DENY: reboot/shutdown — container should not control host power state
DENY: Raw I/O port access — no direct hardware access
DENY: swapon/swapoff — prevent container from manipulating host swap
DENY: pivot_root — prevent container from changing root filesystem
DENY: mount with MS_MOVE — prevent mount namespace escape
DENY: umount2 with MNT_FORCE/MNT_DETACH on restricted paths
DENY: acct — prevent BSD process accounting manipulation
DENY: keyctl — prevent kernel keyring manipulation
DENY: perf_event_open — prevent performance monitoring abuse
DENY: bpf — prevent eBPF program loading from container
DENY: userfaultfd — potential side-channel attack vector
DENY: personality — prevent changing execution domain
DENY: ptrace — prevent process tracing (container escape vector)
DENY: mknod — prevent creation of device nodes

RESTRICT ioctl: Allow only binder ioctl commands. Binder ioctl magic is 'b' (0x62). BINDER_WRITE_READ=0xc0306201, BINDER_SET_MAX_THREADS=0x40046205, BINDER_SET_CONTEXT_MGR=0x40046207, BINDER_THREAD_EXIT=0x40046208, BINDER_VERSION=0xc0046209, BINDER_GET_NODE_DEBUG_INFO=0xc050620b, BINDER_GET_NODE_INFO_FOR_REF=0xc050620c, BINDER_SET_CONTEXT_MGR_EXT=0x4010620d, BINDER_FREEZE=0x4010620e, BINDER_GET_FROZEN_INFO=0xc010620f, BINDER_ENABLE_ONEWAY_SPAM_DETECTION=0x40046210. We allow ioctl generally but log non-binder ioctl via SELinux audit. Terminal ioctls (TIOCGWINSZ etc.) are also needed.
syscalls._note: ioctl filtering by fd+cmd is complex in seccomp; granular restriction is enforced via SELinux device access rules and cgroup device ACLs instead. Only /dev/binder and ptys are accessible.

syscalls._comment: DENY: clock_settime — prevent container from changing system clock
DENY: lookup_dcookie — kernel profiling interface
DENY: open_by_handle_at — container escape CVE-2015-1335
DENY: quotactl — prevent disk quota manipulation
DENY: setns — prevent namespace switching (container escape)
DENY: unshare — prevent creating new namespaces inside container
